name: Lightkurve (Kepler/TESS Light Curve Analysis)

auth.notes: Downloads from MAST archive (NASA); no auth for public data

performance.notes: Network-bound for data download; computation is NumPy-based

agent_readiness.notes: Kepler/K2/TESS light curve analysis for exoplanet science. import lightkurve as lk. search_result = lk.search_lightcurve('Kepler-10', mission='Kepler'). lc_collection = search_result.download_all(). lc = lc_collection.stitch(). lc.plot(). lc.flatten(window_length=401). lc.fold(period=0.837, epoch_time=2454833.0). bls = lc.to_periodogram(method='bls'). bls.plot(). planet_b = bls.get_transit_model(). PixelFile: TPF for pixel-level analysis. Correctors: PLDCorrector, SFFCorrector.

gotchas: Requires internet for MAST data download — not for offline analysis
Domain-specific — only for Kepler/K2/TESS stellar photometry
Large download sizes for full mission datasets
BLS periodogram is slow for long time series
best_when: Exoplanet transit detection from Kepler/K2/TESS data
Stellar variability analysis from space photometry
Period search via BLS (Box Least Squares) periodogram
Systematic correction of Kepler/TESS light curves
avoid_when: When MAST download is not possible
summary: Lightkurve provides Kepler/K2/TESS light curve analysis for exoplanet science. lk.search_lightcurve('Kepler-10').download_all().stitch(). lc.flatten().fold(period=...). to_periodogram(method='bls'). MIT by NASA/Geert Barentsen. Standard library for Kepler/TESS exoplanet research. Downloads from MAST archive. BLS transit search. Pixel-level analysis. Systematic correctors (PLD, SFF).
